sowing.method: Planting of budded saplings (on disease-resistant rootstock) in prepared pits

seed_rate.notes: 270-300 plants per hectare under standard 6m x 6m spacing layout.

sowing.soil_conditions: Deep, well-drained, sandy loam soils with pH 5.5-7.5. Good internal drainage is key.

irrigation.notes: Provide regular watering (every 3-5 days) to help establish saplings.
Withhold water for 1-2 months (bahar treatment) to induce uniform flowering.
Provide optimal water. Drought causes leaf drop and fruit splitting.

fertilizer.method: Ring method along the drip line of tree
fertilizer.notes: Apply mixed with well-decomposed manure and zinc sulfate.
Apply Potassium to promote fruit sweetness, size, and skin shine.

maturity_indicators: Skin color changes from dark green to deep orange/yellow-orange
Fruit juice content reaches minimum 35% with optimal sugar-acid ratio
harvest.harvest_method: Hand plucking using clippers to cut stalks
harvest.notes: Do not pull fruits directly as it tears the skin. Treat with fungicide before packing.
